final_answer: According to the information found, Amir-Abbas Hoveyda was the Prime Minister of Iran from January 1965 to August 1977. Therefore, in April 1977, Amir-Abbas Hoveyda was the Prime Minister of Iran.

message.content:
From the search results, we can see that the first place mentioned by name in the Book of Esther in the New International Version is Susa. Now, we need to find out who was the Prime Minister of Iran (or Persia, as it was known then) in April 1977.

Let's search for this information.
<tool_call>
{"name": "web_search", "arguments": {"query": "Prime Minister of Iran in April 1977"}}
</tool_call>

args.query: Prime Minister of Iran in April 1977

response.content:
Title: Amir-Abbas Hoveyda - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amir-Abbas_Hoveyda
Snippet: Amir-Abbas Hoveyda[a] (18 February 1919 - 7 April 1979) [1] was an Iranian economist and politician who served as Prime Minister of Iran from 27 January 1965 to 7 August 1977. He was the longest-serving prime minister in Iran's history, serving in office for over 12 years. He also served as the first Secretary-General of the Rastakhiz Party from 1975 to 1976 and the deputy prime minister and ...
---
Title: Amīr ʿAbbas Hoveyda | Iranian, Shah, Pahlavi | Britannica
URL: https://www.britannica.com/biography/Amir-Abbas-Hoveyda
Snippet: Amīr ʿAbbas Hoveyda (born Feb. 18, 1919, Tehrān—died April 7, 1979, Tehrān) was the prime minister of Iran under Shah Mohammed Reza Pahlavi from January 1965 to August 1977.
---
Title: Historic History of Iran: Amir Abbas Hoveyda - Iran Chamber
URL: https://www.iranchamber.com/history/ahoveyda/amir_abbas_hoveyda.php
Snippet: As leader of the ruling Iran Novin Party, he won a landslide victory at the 1971 general elections. Iran's financial strength enabled Hoveyda to strike advantageous bargains with Western customers for Iranian oil in order to promote Iran's own economic development. But the Shah on August 7, 1977, replaced Hoveyda with Jamshid Amouzegar.
---
Title: List of prime ministers of Iran - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_prime_ministers_of_Iran
Snippet: The office of Prime Minister of Iran was established in 1907 during the Persian Constitutional Revolution and existed until 1989 when the office was abolished after a constitutional referendum. The prime minister was the head of government of Iran. Ali Asghar Khan Atabak was the first and Mir-Hossein Mousavi was the last prime minister of Iran.
---
Title: Government of Amir-Abbas Hoveyda (1974-1977) - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Amir-Abbas_Hoveyda_(1974–1977)
Snippet: The sixth Hoveyda government was a cabinet of Iran led by Prime Minister Amir-Abbas Hoveyda that was formed on 27 May 1974 and presented to Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi on 29 May. Hoveyda reshuffled in the cabinet on 8 November 1976, replacing all of his ministers. The government was dissolved in August 1977 and replaced by that of new Prime Minister Jamshid Amouzegar. [1]
